headlines.text: Iran fires 13 missiles and 17 drones at Kuwait and Bahrain, killing one at Kuwait International Airport
Ceasefire in doubt as Pentagon, State Dept inspectors launch War Powers Act review of 96-day Iran war
Six-state primaries deliver surprise: Iowa Republican voters reject Trump-endorsed gubernatorial candidate
Steve Hilton leads California governor's race; Karen Bass advances to runoff against Spencer Pratt in LA mayor's race
CBS fires Scott Pelley from 60 Minutes after he called new boss Bari Weiss a murderer of the show at a staff meeting
Stocks fall as oil surges past $95 a barrel on fresh Iran-Gulf strikes rattling markets
Trump picks Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence, replacing career intelligence officials
Ukraine drones strike oil terminal in St. Petersburg, setting it ablaze during Putin economic forum
Supreme Court allows Alabama GOP-friendly congressional map to stand, cuts majority-Black district ahead of midterms
SpaceX IPO roadshow kicks off; Morningstar pegs valuation at $780B, half of Elon Musk's ask

breaking.text: BREAKING: Iran fires 13 ballistic missiles and 17 drones at Kuwait and Bahrain; 1 killed at Kuwait airport, US retaliates with strikes on Qeshm Island

stories.hed: Iran Bombs Kuwait Airport, US Bombs Iran Back, Everyone Insists the Ceasefire Is Fine
stories.dek: Iran launched 13 ballistic missiles and 17 drones at Kuwait and Bahrain before dawn, killing one person at Kuwait International Airport and injuring 60-plus — the highest casualty count in an Iranian strike since the war began. The US promptly fired back at a ground control station on Qeshm Island, and the Pentagon inspector general announced it must now legally review the war because it has exceeded 60 days, which is awkward since the administration said it reset the clock in April.

stories.hed: Ukraine Drones Set St. Petersburg Oil Terminal Ablaze Mid-Putin Economic Forum
stories.dek: Ukrainian drones struck and ignited an oil terminal in St. Petersburg on Wednesday while Russia was simultaneously hosting its annual economic showcase in the same city — a spectacular piece of timing that Zelenskyy confirmed. The Strait of Hormuz remains paralyzed and global crude stockpiles just logged their sixth consecutive weekly drop of 8 million barrels.

stories.hed: Iowa Voters Reject Trump Endorsement, Handing First Primary Loss to MAGA Machine in 2026 Midterms
stories.dek: Republican businessman Zach Lahn beat Trump-endorsed Rep. Randy Feenstra in Iowa's gubernatorial primary by less than a percentage point, marking the first time a Trump-backed candidate has lost a governor, House, or Senate primary in this midterm cycle. Six states voted Tuesday and California's governor race between Steve Hilton and Xavier Becerra remains too close to call.

stories.hed: Scott Pelley Fired from 60 Minutes After Calling New Boss Bari Weiss a Show-Murderer at Staff Meeting
stories.dek: Veteran CBS correspondent Scott Pelley publicly lit into new executive producer Nick Bilton and editor-in-chief Bari Weiss at a Monday all-hands meeting, accusing Weiss of murdering the 57-year-old newsmagazine while staff applauded. Bilton fired him the next day in a termination letter citing 'remarkable incivility and contempt,' making Pelley the latest in a string of veteran 60 Minutes talent to exit since Paramount Skydance took over.

stories.hed: Trump Says He Started the Iran War Himself and Also Wants to Meet the New Supreme Leader for Lunch, Probably
stories.dek: When asked if Netanyahu tricked him into Operation Epic Fury, Trump said 'I'm the one that started it' — which is either refreshing candor or the most aggressive own-goal in diplomatic history. He then mused that he'd 'like to meet' Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, declined to rule out the Hormuz blockade lasting through Labor Day, and said the economy is doing 'very well, other than this,' gesturing vaguely at the ongoing war.

markets.context: Stocks slid and oil jumped above $95 as Iran-Gulf strikes reignited fears that the fragile ceasefire is unraveling and the Strait of Hormuz blockade could drag into summer.

gas.note: Paying $4.26 a gallon to sit in traffic is the real weapon of mass destruction.

popCulture.hed: House of the Dragon Season 3 Arrives June 21, The Bear's Final Season and Love Island USA Also Premiering This Month
popCulture.dek: June is stacked: HBO's penultimate Targaryen civil war season drops June 21, The Bear bows out for good, and Love Island USA returns with Ariana Madix hosting a new batch of singles in Fiji. Toy Story 5 hits theaters June 19, and Olivia Rodrigo drops a new album June 12, meaning your FYP will be completely uninhabitable by mid-month.

genZ.term: Charli XCX Rock Music glitch edit
genZ.definition: Charli XCX dropped 'Rock Music' on May 7 and the internet latched onto a deliberate vocal glitch mid-track as the mechanic for a viral video format where creators freeze-frame or stutter their footage to match the malfunction. It has become the dominant editing format flooding TikTok FYPs heading into June, spawning thousands of brand and creator executions.
genZ.usage: POV: the Iran ceasefire doing the Charli XCX glitch edit every 48 hours.

tldr: Iran bombed Kuwait's airport and US fired back at Qeshm Island on Day 96 of a war the Pentagon now legally has to investigate, while stocks fell and oil topped $95. Back home, Iowa voters handed Trump his first primary endorsement loss of the midterm cycle and Scott Pelley got fired from 60 Minutes for saying out loud what everyone was thinking.
